decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. The current value (500.0) is at the upper bound defined in the editable variables, but the strategy note identifies it as the 'Best first-line lever'. Assuming the physical material limit allows for improvement or the bound was a previous step limit, I will attempt a moderate increase. However, since 500.0 is explicitly listed as the bound in the prompt's editable variables section, I cannot exceed it without violating the 'Bounds' rule. Re-reading the rules: 'Bounds=[20.0, 500.0]'. Since the current value is exactly at the upper bound, I cannot increase conductivity further. I must pivot to the next highest priority lever: geometry. I will increase the heat spreader width (currently 0.9) to maximize lateral spreading area. The bound is 1.0, so I can increase it to 1.0.

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader width increases the lateral area available for heat dissipation towards the Dirichlet boundaries, helping to lower the peak chip temperature further.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
risk_notes: Approaching the design domain width limit; future geometry changes may be constrained
Diminishing returns as the spreader approaches the full domain width
